response:
<> To replace one URL with another within a string in PHP you can use the str_replace function. This approach will work for multiple instances of the same URL in the string. Here is an example of how to achieve this ```php $string = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $updatedString = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $string); echo $updatedString; ``` This code replaces all occurrences of the old URL with the new URL in the string. The result will be as expected with the updated links. A palindrome is a word or phrase that reads the same backward as forward. An example is "madam" or "racecar".
